message:
test(readme): cover 13 more StringAccessor methods (fd90.248 / br-4zyhp)

Extends readme_string_accessor_compiles_and_runs with 13 more methods:

- contains_any(&[patterns]) — true if any matches
- count_literal — exact substring count (vs count_matches regex)
- encode("utf-8") / decode("utf-8") — pass-through round-trip
- expandtabs(4) — replace \t with N spaces
- strip_chars(" *") — strip arbitrary chars from both ends
- slice_replace(start, stop, repl) — substring replacement
- replace_regex_all(pat, repl) — global replace (vs replace_regex)
- extract_to_frame(named-capture pattern) — DataFrame of captures
- split_expand(",") — DataFrame with one col per split position
- wrap(8) — wrap each row to a max width
- translate("abc", "ABC") — char-by-char substitution table
- str().get_dummies("|") — sep-separated tag string → one-hot DataFrame

Combined with fd90.194 (26 methods) + fd90.247 (17 methods), ~56 of
the 50+ documented StringAccessor methods are now exercised.

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -134,6 +134,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-4r5t5","title":"fd90.124: README error table shows LengthMismatch as unit variant but actual is struct in both FrameError and IndexError","description":"fd90.74-123 README audit — Error Architecture table at lines 821-830. Two rows list 'LengthMismatch' as if it were a unit variant, alongside other struct-variant entries that correctly use braces:

  | IndexError | ... | OutOfBounds { position, length }, LengthMismatch, ... |
  | FrameError | ... | LengthMismatch, DuplicateIndexUnsupported, ... |

But both are struct variants:
- crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:111 — FrameError::LengthMismatch { index_len, column_len }
- crates/fp-index/src/lib.rs:1344 — IndexError::LengthMismatch { expected, actual, context }

The README has been progressively normalized (see fd90.101 for MissingIndexColumn). 'LengthMismatch' is the last remaining unit-typed-looking entry that's actually a struct variant.

Also: TypeError row shows IncompatibleDtypes { left, right } correctly with braces, so the README convention is established — the LengthMismatch entries just weren't updated.

Fix:
- IndexError row: LengthMismatch → LengthMismatch { expected, actual, context }
- FrameError row: LengthMismatch → LengthMismatch { index_len, column_len }

Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:38:58.855538828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:40:13.784292249Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:40:13.781684259Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-4vno","title":"fp-io SQL query builder helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: extract and test backend-neutral SQL query builder helpers for SELECT projections and write-side CREATE/INSERT statement construction. Keep this separate from pane 3's Postgres/schema adapter work; no new files. Reuse existing identifier validation/escaping and parameter_marker/type mapping hooks, add focused fp-io tests for table SELECT, projected SELECT, CREATE table with named index, backend-specific insert markers, and invalid identifiers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cod-pandas-4","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:54.714604899Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:32:08.639397670Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:32:08.637399347Z","close_reason":"Scope completed in parallel via fd90.x sub-beads on cc-pandas-2: backend-neutral SQL query builder helpers ship as sql_select_all_query_in_schema (fd90.14), sql_select_columns_query_in_schema (fd90.34), sql_create_table_query_in_schema (fd90.16), sql_insert_rows_query_in_schema (fd90.15), sql_multi_row_insert_query_in_schema (fd90.19), sql_drop_table_query_in_schema (fd90.16). All route through identifier validation + conn.quote_identifier + conn.parameter_marker. Tests cover bare-table SELECT, projected SELECT, schema-qualified CREATE/INSERT, backend marker overrides, and invalid identifier rejection (sql_select_all_query_*, sql_create_table_query_*, sql_insert_rows_query_*, write_sql_* tests in fp-io). cod-pandas-4 was stalled since 2026-04-25 (cod agents at usage limits until 2026-04-30); reassigning to cc-pandas-2 retroactively to reflect actual completion.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-4zyhp","title":"fd90.248: Cover remaining StringAccessor methods (decode/encode/expandtabs/wrap/etc.)","description":"fd90.248: Cover remaining StringAccessor methods (~10 more).

After fd90.194 + fd90.247 (~43 methods), still uncovered:
- contains_any / startswith_any / endswith_any
- count_literal, count_matches
- decode / encode
- expandtabs
- extract_df / extract_to_frame
- replace_regex_all (replace_regex covered)
- slice_replace
- split_df, split_df_n, split_expand, split_expand_n, rsplit_get, rsplit_df, partition_df, rpartition_df, split_regex_get
- strip_chars, rstrip_chars
- translate, wrap, wrap_with_drop_whitespace
- get_dummies (str variant)

Scope: Add ~10 more representative methods.

 {"id":"frankenpandas-50ng9","title":"fd90.166: add Sorting integration test (sort_values + sort_values_na + sort_index)","description":"fd90.74-165 follow-on. The README's 'Sorting' section (lines 1192-1206) hasn't been integration-tested. It exercises:
  - df.sort_values('column', ascending: bool)?
  - series.sort_values_na(ascending, na_position: &str)?  -- 'first' / 'last' for NaN placement
  - df.sort_index(ascending: bool)?

Fix: add 'readme_sorting_compiles_and_runs' which mirrors the example. Build a small DataFrame with a numeric column + NaN, sort by column ascending/descending, build a Series with NaN and verify sort_values_na NaN placement, and sort the DataFrame by index. Pure additive test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:22:29.876331490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:24:39.699251430Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:24:39.696390271Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-5197l","title":"fd90.206: Add SqlReadOptions/SqlWriteOptions/SqlInspector/read_sql_chunks to prelude","description":"fd90.206: Add SQL helper types + read_sql_chunks family to the prelude.

The README's IO Format Support table at line 148 documents the SQL row with these types:
- read_sql / read_sql_table (already in prelude)
- read_sql_chunks (NOT in prelude — exposed at line 117)
- SqlReadOptions (params, parse_dates, coerce_float, dtype, schema, columns, index_col) — NOT in prelude (line 80)
- SqlWriteOptions (if_exists, index, index_label, schema, dtype, method, chunksize) — NOT in prelude (line 84)
- SqlInspector for SQLAlchemy.Inspector-shaped introspection — NOT in prelude (line 78)

User code following the README that calls `df.read_sql_chunks(&conn, "SELECT ...", chunk_size)?` or builds a `SqlReadOptions { params: ..., parse_dates: ... }` cannot do so via `use frankenpandas::prelude::*;` alone — they need explicit `use frankenpandas::{SqlReadOptions, SqlWriteOptions, SqlInspector, read_sql_chunks};`.

Scope:
- Add to prelude: SqlReadOptions, SqlWriteOptions, SqlInspector, read_sql_chunks (plus the *_with_* variants for symmetry with the existing read_sql)
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard
- Per scope discipline: only add the named types (don't sweep in unrelated SQL helpers)

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.
Time estimate: 15-25min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T21:24:18.265727008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T21:27:01.916117238Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T21:27:01.913356319Z","close_reason":"Added 4 SQL helpers to the prelude — SqlReadOptions, SqlWriteOptions, SqlInspector, read_sql_chunks. All four are documented in the README's IO Format Support table at line 148 (SQL row mentions 'SqlReadOptions (params, parse_dates, ...)', 'SqlWriteOptions (if_exists, index, ...)', 'SqlInspector for SQLAlchemy.Inspector-shaped introspection') but were exposed at the top-level only. Extended prelude_completeness_compile_guard with type-checks for each (SqlInspector via fn-pointer over &SqlInspector<'_, rusqlite::Connection>; SqlWriteOptions has no Default impl, used fn-pointer). All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-52lj","title":"pandas: Series.reset_index(drop=False) DataFrame-return parity","description":"py signature:
pd.Series([1,2], name="x").reset_index(drop=False) -> DataFrame with index + value columns

Current fp-frame rejects this path in crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs around Series::reset_index(): the method returns CompatibilityRejected with a hint to call to_frame()?.reset_index(false) instead of matching pandas directly. Add native parity for the drop=false widening case, including unnamed-series column naming and index-name collision behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:56:14.120578035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:23.590318907Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:23.588461529Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
